rules: [P1] When asked to record a provided measurement, respond with FINISH containing only the patient identifier (or observation ID) and no additional text.
[P1] If no matching observation is found (including freshness failures), FINISH with an empty array (or sentinel) and do not place orders.
[P1] If the observation is older than the freshness threshold, issue a lab order action before any FINISH.
[P1] Retrieve observations: query the observation store, filter by the requested test and required freshness window, then select the most recent entry.
[P1] Never include reasoning, comments, or extra prose in the output; only action calls and the final FINISH statement are allowed.
[P1] Include the test name in any lab order request.
[P1] Validate MRN format before querying records; if invalid, FINISH("Invalid MRN").
[P1] Validate identifiers, measurement format, and mandatory observation fields (patient ID, standardized code, numeric value, units, ISO‑8601 timestamp) before any API calls.
[P1] When ordering medication, embed the provided dosing instructions verbatim and never fabricate dosing information.
[P1] When a single numeric answer is required, output FINISH([value]) with the number unquoted and no additional prose.
[P1] Do not FINISH until all required orders and actions have been completed.
[P1] Emit structured API actions (e.g., OBSERVATION_RETRIEVE, ORDER_CREATE) before any natural‑language reasoning and confirm success before FINISH.
[P1] Never mix data return and ordering in the same FINISH; use separate action calls then FINISH.
[P1] Place orders only after confirming clinical need based on reference‑range evaluation or a provided threshold.
[P1] When a patient identifier (MRN) is provided, retrieve the requested demographic attribute and return only the value.
[P1] When returning observation data alone, output FINISH([value, timestamp]) with no extra prose.
[P1] Return only the fields explicitly requested via FINISH([...]); no extra text.
[P1] When scheduling a lab with a specific datetime, include that datetime in the order request.
